title: Scroll / Observation
summary: Web-platform prior art grounding backlog #014's 'project vs intent vs neither' call for the scroll surface. The platform ships no 'scroll domain' — just independent primitives sharing a scrollbar: IntersectionObserver (the in-view trigger), scroll-driven animations (animation-timeline — a motion concern), scroll-snap (layout), content-visibility (the virtualization floor), History.scrollRestoration (navigation), scrollend. So scroll earns neither a project nor a monolithic intent; decompose onto native seams that mostly already have homes. The one reusable residue is a thin viewport-presence mechanism intent owning the observe-vocabulary (root/rootMargin/threshold/enter-leave) that three existing IntersectionObserver consumers compose — Collection Operations' advance:auto, Prefetch's eagerness:viewport, and visibility-gated trait activation. advance:auto stays a Collection Operations dimension and composes that trigger, resolving the dated seam with one home for the trigger, three for the intent.
